observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed, to require the:
Municipal Treasurer to prepare and provide copy of the list of unreleased checks to the Municipal Accountant at the end of the year and secure a copy of the bank statement of LBP Real Tacloban 1732-1187-36 account, (PMNP-HPG)
Municipal Accountant to:
To restore back to the CIB and the corresponding payable accounts the amount of unreleased checks at year-end;
Make an adjusting journal entry for the cancelled checks and identified reconciling items;
Prepare and submit monthly the BRS of all its depository bank accounts, take up reconciling items; and
Prepare BRS for LBP Real Tacloban 1732-1187-36 account, (PMNP-HPG).

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed, to contemplate insuring insurable LGU properties under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002, taking into consideration the financial capacity and practicality of taking property insurance.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed, to direct the:
Municipal Treasurer and Budget Officer to strengthen internal controls to ensure that all lawful expenditures and obligations are properly accounted for at year-end; and
The Municipal Accountant to maintain recorded Accounts Payable and related expense accounts at a manageable level to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the reported balances.
Henceforth, implement a year-end cutoff to ensure the accurate recording of Accounts Payable and related Expenses account.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed, to require the:
Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies and materials in its appropriate inventory account as required under COA Circular No. 2015-009 and to maintain the Perpetual Inventory Method; and
The Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the monthly SSMI reports and the semi-annual RPCI; and conduct a physical count of all unissued or undelivered inventory items; and submit results to both the Accounting Office and the Audit Team.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the GAD Focal Person:
Include the HGDG training in the CY 2025 GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) as mandated by item 5(b) of Section 4.1.C.4 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, if necessary; and
Use the HGDG test in determining the attribution rate of the GAD fund in the LGU’s various Programs, Projects, and Activities on the succeeding GPBs and GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Provide the Audit Team with copies of documents that support the recorded Prior Period Errors and Adjustment of net revenue recognized directly in net assets/ equity;
Identify the nature of the adjustments/errors to determine the extent of the error if it requires prospective or retrospective restatement of the FS; and
Fully disclose the Prior Period Errors in the Notes to Financial Statements, as required by Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed, to direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the DVs and supporting documents for the above-mentioned transactions, and henceforth, ensure that all DVs and supporting documents for transactions paid through auto-debit arrangement are submitted on a regular and timely basis.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed. to exercise prudence in hiring and utilizing the services of the JOs;
We likewise, recommended that LCE direct the:
Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to prepare the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan in order to assess the required number of JO workers to be hired based on necessity and financial capability of the municipality; and,
Municipal Budget Officer to properly and regularly monitor the budget utilization to serve as guide of the Sanggunian Bayan in the preparation of the Supplemental Budget.

(5) Implementing Department Heads, to implement the programs, projects and activities as intended within the agreed timelines;

(8) We recommended, and the Management agreed that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to include the necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements for Property, Plant and Equipment account as prescribed in IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2016-004.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that the LCE direct both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:

(14) We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately the cash corresponding the understatement of TL-LDRRMF account in the amount  of ₱124,661.14 .

(16) We also recommended that the LGU strictly comply with the regulations provided in RA 9184 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-03 on the grant of BAC honoraria involved in government procurement.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the:

ii.	(30 )Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the balances of unexpended LDRRMF as shown in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for fair presentation and to enhance the understandability of the users of financial statements.

(39) recommend the suspension of the salaries of accountable officers who continuously fail to submit their liquidation reports; and
 
  (40) reconcile the unaccounted amount of P153,888.28

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Require the accountable officers, especially those with long overdue cash
advances, and those outgoing officers and employees, before issuance of
clearance certificate, to immediately settle or refund their unliquidated
cash advance;
b. Regularly update monitoring of all cash advances and strictly implement
the non-granting of additional cash advance to officers and employees, and
issue demand letters to AO with outstanding cash advance. as of December
31, 2023;
c. File a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts with
complete necessary supporting documents, if warranted for CA involving
AO who have already died and/or absconded;
d. Prepare individual subsidiary ledgers for each AO with proper disclosure
or classification as to whether they are still employed, have resigned or
deceased, in order for the Municipality to properly plan possible course of
action to undertake in the settlement of cash advances.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that the LCE direct the:
a. Implementing Department Heads, to implement the programs, projects
and activities as intended within the agreed timelines;
b. Municipal Treasurer, to return all unexpended or excess funds to SA; and
c. Municipal Accountant, to maintain a subsidiary for each received fund
received and diligently pursuing efforts and remedies to ascertain
specific details regarding the unsubstantiated balance under GF and
reconciling items under TF.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that the LCE direct the
Municipal Accountant to include the necessary disclosures in the Notes to
Financial Statements for Property, Plant and Equipment account as
prescribed in IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2016-004.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that the LCE direct both the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Conduct a reconciliation of the prior years’ outstanding balance of
₱302,534.21; and
b. Remit immediately the unremitted balance for CY 2023 of ₱67,168.09
along with the reconciled balance of Due to BIR account as of December
31, 2023

Moving forward, to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of
Presidential Decree No. 1445 and BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98, by
remitting on time fund due to BIR to avoid the incurrence of interest,
penalties and/or surcharges.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that the LCE direct both the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Reconcile the prior years’ outstanding balance of ₱353,934.45; and
b. Remit immediately the reconciled balance of GSIS, PhilHealth and Due to
Pag-IBIG accounts as of December 31, 2023.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to that LGU officials ensure
the appropriate utilization of the 5% LDRRMF exclusively for programs,
projects, and activities directly related to disaster management and mitigation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately
the cash corresponding the understatement of TL-LDRRMF account in the
amount of ₱124,661.14 .

We recommended, and the Management agreed that the LGU officials ensure
that the projects charged under the 20% Development Fund are in compliance
with the provision of Item No. 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, dated
November 4, 2020.

We also recommended that the LGU strictly comply with the regulations
provided in RA 9184 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-03 on the grant of
BAC honoraria involved in government procurement.

1.14. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
c. The Municipal Assessor to submit up-to-date Assessment Roll to the Municipal
Treasurer and the former to furnish a copy to the Municipal Accountant as the
basis for set-up the RPT and SET Receivables; and
d. The Municipal Accountant set-up its RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of
the year and analyze the proposed adjusting/correcting entries in the books to
rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the RPT and SET
Receivables and its related accounts.
1.15. Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant should follow the correct accounting
entries in order to reflect the correct amount of affected accounts in the financial
statements.

2.12 We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a.) Analyze the proposed adjusting/correcting entries in the books to rectify
the noted understatement of the due to NGAs account and its related
accounts;
b.) Identify the reason of the variance based on the disclosed schedule and make
the necessary adjusting entries and provide complete and sufficient
information in the Notes to Financial Statements to give additional value to
the financial statements of the LGU for relevant decision-making of its users
and other readers;
c.) Provide necessary certification that the funds previously transferred to
Implementing Agency (IA) has been liquidated and accounted for in the
books.
2.13 Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant should follow the correct
accounting entries in order to reflect the correct amount of affected accounts
in the financial statements

3.9 We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the:
1. The Municipal Assessor to act on the processing of the TCTs of the parcels
of land reported under the name of the Municipality, and to register them
with the Register of Deeds without any further delay;
2. The Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for all depreciable
PPE; and
3. The Inventory Committee:
i. to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA
Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of
inventory-taking activities; and
ii. ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as
provided for in COA Circular 2020-006.

4.5 We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
provide sufficient information in the Notes to Financial Statements for long-
term Loan Payable to give additional value to the financial statements of the
LGU for relevant decision-making of its users and other readers.

5.14 We recommended and management agreed that the LCE direct the:
d. MHO to:
1. Record all donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the (1) Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily in the required form, and (3) the inventory of remaining undistributed items, moving forward;
2. Submit a copy of the same to the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant within the reglementary period.
e. Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Assist the MHO in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-in-
kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed
items, moving forward; and
2. Maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received,
Distributed and Balances and its supporting documents.
3. Prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.
f. Municipal Accountant to:
1. Disclose in the Notes to FS the quantity received, issued and balance
of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization
as to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and
others.

6.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
b. Municipal Engineer to immediately act on the unimplemented and delayed
projects; and
b. Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement and implementation
process to realize the programmed projects as planned.
6.8 Moving forward, we recommended that the management optimally utilize the 20%
DF and ensure that development projects are well-planned and are
procurement-and-implementation-ready.

7.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately send collection letters to the delinquent LGU Officials
and provide a copy of their payments to this Office; and
7.11 We likewise recommended that the legislative officials, in consultation with the
MT and other concerned officials to draw up plans to strategize and strengthen
collection drive beyond the required posting and sending of notices.

8.5 We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to release the
respective barangay share immediately; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the actual share of the province and
barangays from the RPT collections.

9.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
c. LDRRMO to cause the inclusion of the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF in the LDRRMFIP; and
d. Municipal Accountant to:
1. Identify the cause or reason of the variance and make the
necessary adjusting entries, if any, to be able to present fairly
the balance of the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF account in the
Financial Statement;
2. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the
balances of unexpended LDRRMF as shown in Annex D of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 for fair presentation and to enhance the
understandability of the users of financial statements.
9.14 Moving forward, we likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive
require the strict observance of the accounting and reporting guidelines for
the LDRRMF as enunciated under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

10.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit the required certification
and to direct the human resource personnel to submit all individual contracts for
each JO personnel which contain the specific job description and the specific office
to where he/she is assigned and rates on a daily basis.

1.9 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Initiate the request for authorization to use the unspent trust fund from
DAR Central Office and Province of Samar for any priority projects of
the LGU; and
b. Draw a JEV to return the Trust Fund as LGU’s counterpart to General
Fund.

2.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Create an Inventory Committee as required by COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31, 2020;
b. Create a position for MGSO if fund warrants or otherwise, the Municipal
Treasurer shall assume the duty of the MGSO; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to review and draw adjusting entries
for the discrepancies in the PPE accounts.

2.11 However, the LGU will only designate a MGSO instead of creating plantilla
position due to fund limitation.

3.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. require the focal person/unit in custody with the inventories to submit RIS
and SSMI to Accounting Unit on a regular basis to recognize the related
issuances; and
b. henceforth, recognize purchases of inventories as Inventory Accounts
instead of being directly recognized as Expense Accounts.

3.11 The Municipal Accountant will review the procedure on the issuance and
maintaining of inventory accounts. The Audit Team has emphasized that purchases
of inventories, properties and capitalizable items should be recorded as asset
accounts. Any issuances to end-users should be properly documented. Issuance of
supplies or inventories should be documented using the prescribed forms hence
timely accounting of related expenses could be booked up the books of accounts.

4.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Prepare an adjusting journal entry on the erroneous entries; and
b. Prepare and maintain the required Subsidiary Ledgers for proper
monitoring of the account balances.

4.10 The MA confirmed that she maintained the schedule of accounts, however
subsidiary ledgers were not complied.

5.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant that henceforth to properly account the
accounting transactions; draw a JEV to adjust the erroneous recognition
of Medical Equipment account and avoid processing payments with
incomplete documentation and/or supporting documents; and
b. Instruct the BAC Secretariat through the BAC Chairperson to promptly
post the procurement related documents to PhilGEPS website and prepare
the Purchase Orders or its equivalent for every successful procurement
activity.

5.12 The LCE instructed the accountant to discuss the matter with the in-charge of the
medical supplies to improve the procedures on handling medical supplies. The
Audit Team reiterate to recognize purchases of supplies, inventories and property
to its respective asset accounts. Likewise, the BAC Secretariat confirmed that they
missed to post required award documents to the portal of PhilGEPS.

6.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Ensure the immediate implementation of the PPAs funded from the 20%
Development Fund;
b. Require the Municipal Planning Development Committee to review
developmental projects that are feasible for implementation and ensure
that the programs, projects and activities are well-planned and
procurement-and-implementation ready;
c. Re-align the funds appropriated for projects that are not feasible for
implementation;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned to
strictly adhere with the provisions of DILG and DOF JMC No. 1 on the
allowable expenditures out of 20% DF; and
e. Instruct the BAC Secretariat through the BAC Chairperson to post all
necessary procurement related documents to PhilGEPS website as
required under the RA No. 9184.

6.13 The management has stressed out that the projects were implemented and were not
only paid as of CY 2021 however were already completed and paid in January 2022.
In addition, the management will look into the possible realignment of projects that
were long outstanding of not being implemented to another priority programs and
projects. The contributory reasons for the unimplemented projects were due to
COVI-19 pandemic hence delays occurred.

7.20 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
1. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. properly take up asset accounts upon receipts of inventories,
supplies and/or equipment;
b. strictly comply on a timely manner the suggested adjusting journal
entries in the corresponding books of accounts; and
c. refrain from processing disbursement vouchers or payment claims
with incomplete or with deficient attachments.
2. Instruct the BAC Chairperson to:
a. orient the BAC Members and BAC Secretariat the compliance of
RA No. 9184 and henceforth to meticulously hire consultant with
profound expertise on the field industry which the LGU needs to
avoid possible additional costs or losses on the part of the LGU; and
b. inform the BAC Secretariat to properly fill out procurement related
form and contracts.

7.21 The Municipal Accountant pointed out that the goods for distribution were directly
distributed to affected individuals hence the purchase of relief goods were
outrightly expensed out. She also explained that the reason for non-transfer of
unexpended LDRRM Fund was due to dormancy of Trust Fund bank account
where the LGU needs to request first the bank to reactivate the account through
Sanggunian Bayan’s resolution.

8.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
1. Instruct the Administrative Officer or HRMO to:
a. meticulously calculate the accumulated leave credit balance of every
officer and employee; and
b. submit to the Audit Team the Leave Cards and other supporting
documents on the terminal leave credit grants.
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. initiate the recovery through sending demand letter to the retirees
with excess payments of TLB, if warranted;
b. draw an adjusting entry to book up the unpaid TLB; and
c. henceforth, book up Leave Benefits Payable for retirees with
accumulated leave credits, if any, in the books of accounts on the
year of retirement.

8.11 The HRMO commented that the elective officials are exempted from the mandatory
annual five (5) days vacation leave under CSC Memorandum Circular No. 06 series
of 2003. The same circular provides that the elective official should present written
manifestation addressed to the HRMO that such elective official wishes not to avail
the mandatory leave. Hence, the HRMO is required to provide the Audit Team the
copy of the written manifestation of such elective official who was not being
deducted by the said mandatory leave prior to the release of the TLB.

9.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to :
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant not to process payments with
incomplete documentation and//or supporting documents;
b. Instruct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to submit
certification of eligible PHWs for the period September 15, 2020 to
December 19, 2020 to the Audit Team; and
c. Require the concerned PHWs to submit their DTRs and Accomplishment
Reports to the Audit Team.

9.13 The required documents will be provided to the Audit Team for further verification
and validation of the financial transactions.

We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which are considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit the lacking contract documents and henceforth, adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR, including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, proper evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the submission of complete contract documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant secure the list required in Section 248 of RA 7160 for the period 2012 to 2016 and prepare adjusting entries to correct the balances for the RPT and SET Receivables. Henceforth, a duly certified list showing all the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible should be prepared by the Municipal Assessor and submitted to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for recording by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor did not submit the list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Office be required to consolidate such requirements and for the BAC to conduct a quarterly bidding thereof.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the understatement of the Cash in Bank account at year-end.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the
Municipal Accountant to verify all unidentified reconciling items and the
stale checks and immediately prepare the necessary adjustments in the books
to avoid its repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS. Moreover, submit
supporting documents on credit adjustments in the General Ledger of the
Trust Fund to validate the adjusting entries made in the books and in the
bank reconciliation.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
prepare an adjusting entry to correct the recorded depreciation on the
LGU’s road networks for fair presentation of accounts in the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
1. The Municipal Assessor to determine the cost of the road lot component
for each road project of the LGU for recognition in the financial
statements;
2. The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components of road projects.
3. The Municipal Accountant to maintain the Local Road Network Ledger
Cards for each component of the local road network.
4. The General Services Office to complete Property Cards with the
necessary data.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the
Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to take up the
bookkeeping error so as to the bring the account to its desired balance of
P10,500.03 and cause the preparation of the remittance list.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. monitor the status of cash advances on a monthly basis;
b. prepare demand letters (for signature of the LCE) and issue to all
officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances;
c. recommend the suspension of the salaries of accountable officers who
continuously fail to submit their liquidation reports.
d. stop the grant of additional cash advances to employees with previous
unliquidated cash advances; and
e. reconcile the unaccounted amount of P153,888.28

We recommended that the LCE oblige:
1. The Municipal Accountant (MA) to:
a. adjust the erroneous remittances made on the Due to BIR account;
b. prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers and closely monitor the monthly
preparation of the taxes withheld and due for remittance;
c. submit the lacking disbursement vouchers (DVs); and
d. verify the balance of P446,271.58 as of December 31, 2019 and
prepare immediately the disbursement voucher for the taxes due to
BIR.
2. The Municipal Treasurer (MT) to:
a. immediately transfer to Trust Fund, the amount of P403,539.92
representing withheld taxes from General Fund books that were paid
under the Trust Fund; and
b. strictly comply with the remittance of creditable withholding taxes on
or before the 10th day of the following month the withholding was
made.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis
and the LDRRMO to submit the said report to the Audit Team on the
required date, pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that henceforth the LCE expedite the implementation of
the project/s programmed under SWMP to ensure the protection of the
public health and environment in the municipality and also not to deprive
the constituents of the benefits that can be derived from the implementation of the
project/s.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan come up with clear policy
guidelines on the hiring of personnel under Job Order Contracts and the
LGU to strictly implement the guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are correctly
computed and deducted from compensation income of municipal officials
and employees, and remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) within
the reglementary period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to
strictly observe the proper reporting of accountable forms using the
appropriate template, and on-time submission of the CRAAF.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MA) be required to
closely monitor the submission by the MT of the report of collection and
deposits with the CRAAF to facilitate the conduct of the required monthly
reconciliation of accounting records and treasury records.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the General Services
Officer accomplish the following:
(a) complete the conduct of inventory of the LGU’s local roads;
(b) prepare an accurate Inventory of Local Roads, and;
(c) submit the Inventory of Local Roads to DILG immediately and every three
years thereafter, without need for demand from the said Office.

We also recommended that the LCE confirm the compliance by the
Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer with the provisions of
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-52 and RA No. 7160 relative to the
above-enumerated specific recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (1) submit the Journal Entry Vouchers with General Journals supporting documents to validate all entries made and make adjusting entries for accounts not transferred to General Fund.

We further recommended (7) to investigate the cause of delay in the payment of loan amortization for the first semester of the calendar year 2018, and require responsible person to refund the P14,493.36 penalty imposed by MDFO, if necessary, and henceforth, ensure that all obligations are paid on time to avoid penalties and surcharges.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the Journal Entry
Vouchers with supporting documents to validate all entries made and make
adjusting entries for accounts not transferred to General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
1.) The Accountant to draw a journal entry to adjust the recorded cost of the
donated Municipal Building based on concrete documents showing the
cost, such as; the program of work with the detailed costing or a deed of
donation.
2.) The Inventory Committee and the Accountant to reconcile the inventory
report against the accounting records for the identified discrepancy
amounting to P66,634,010.25.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to:
1.) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to adjust the overstatement of
P110,885.24 from erroneous recording of the loan transactions in CY 2017
and 2018;
2.) reconcile and adjust the unaccounted prior year understatement of
P240,675.43; and
3.) provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements, the
relevant information relating thereto which are not presented in the face of
the Statement of Financial Position.

We further recommended to investigate the cause of delay in the payment of
loan amortization for the first semester of the calendar year 2018, and require
responsible person to refund the P14,493.36 penalty imposed by MDFO, if
necessary, and henceforth, ensure that all obligations are paid on time to avoid
penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Accountant to file a
request for write-off of the receivable account balances which are considered
dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular No.
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all its Revenue Collection
Clerks to remit to the Municipal Treasurer the full amount of their collections
on the day that it was collected, regardless of the nature of collections made.

We also, recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her
collections as well as collections turned over to her by the collectors daily or on
the next banking day, particularly when collections reach P10,000.00, pursuant
to Section 69(1) of PD 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit the lacking
contract documents and henceforth, adhere to the bidding process prescribed
under RA 9184 and its IRR, including the proper dissemination of bid
opportunities and its result, the conduct of a strict evaluation of bids until the
post qualification activity, and the submission of complete contract documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineering Office /
General Service Office to submit the approved suspension and resumption
order, if any, or an explanation for the delay in delivery of goods, supplies and
materials or project completion, otherwise collect the corresponding amount of
liquidated damages from the supplier/contractor. Likewise, require the
Inspection Officer and the Property Officer to properly fill up the necessary
information in the Inspection and Acceptance Report.

We recommended that the Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to
withhold retention money from progress payments and ensure that only claims
with complete supporting documents are processed and paid. Likewise, require
the BAC and Municipal Accountant to oblige the concerned suppliers and
contractors to post warranty securities for the procurement of goods and
infrastructure projects that had already been fully paid. Henceforth, strictly
observe the mandatory posting of warranty security to guarantee that the
contractor shall perform his responsibilities pursuant to Section 62 of the IRR
of RA 9184 and Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for CY
2018 and prior years which still remained in the General Fund. Also, require
the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of P 1,176,556.53 in the Trust
Liability-LDRRMF Account and disclose the information of the LDRRMF -
Special Trust Account in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the LCE require:
1) The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to intensify efforts to have
the revised SWMP approved by the NSWMC and ensure the proper
implementation.
2) All BSWM Committee prepare BSWM Plan; and
3) The personnel concerned to expedite the processing of land title in the
name of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Sanggunian facilitate
the approval and implementation of the updated Revenue Code to improve the
revenue generation of the municipality in order to fund other Program,
Projects and Activities.

We recommended that the LGU prepare a Contract of Service for each
emergency worker and expedite the formulation of internal policy on the
implementation of Biometric Machine / Bundy Clock as the official register to
record daily attendance. Further, require the immediate submission of the
liquidation reports and supporting documents for all cash advances already
accounted for in the books as liquidated.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
ensure the withholding of taxes on all its payments to suppliers/sellers who are
subjected to 1% or 2% EWT, 5% GVAT and 3% GPT and remit the same to
the BIR on time.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and
Treasurer to verify the composition of the beginning balances of the Due to GSIS
accounts under GF and remit immediately the amount of P508,558.57 due to
GSIS as of December 31, 2018.

Henceforth, remit to GSIS the employees’ and government agency’s
contributions, as well as the loan amortizations promptly, pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 8291 of its IRR.

We recommended that management, thru the representation of the Municipal
Health Officer, strictly follow the guidelines on the allocation and utilization of
Per Family Payment Rate (PFPR) as prescribed in PhilHealth Circular No. 10
s. 2012, and comply with the provisions of the same Circular to avoid ineligible
charges to the Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the GAD Focal
Point System of the LGU:
(e) Conduct a thorough assessment in identifying gender issues and
corresponding activities that directly promote gender equality and women
empowerment through the use of Harmonized Gender and Development
Guidelines (HGDG) tool and to strictly adhere with the provisions of PCW-
NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01;
(f) Submit the agency’s CY 2018 GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit
Team;
(g) Plan adequately its GAD Activities to ensure that expenses adhere to the
principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government;
and
(h) Submit the lacking documentary requirements in the conduct of GAD and
Women Protection Laws Training Workshop.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource
Management Office to: (i) introduce the use of biometrics machine as the official
register to record daily attendance of all personnel of the municipality; (ii)
prepare monthly report of undertimes and absences in order to monitor
compliance with the CSC policies on government office hours; and (iii) effect
the updating of leave cards based thereon, to avoid the possible loss of
government funds in the form of overpayment of leave benefits.

We recommended that the (1) Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of remittances by the Municipal Treasurer, to collecting officers to the encashment of agency checks out of cash collections.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all programs, projects and activities to be funded by the 20% Development Fund are implemented during the budget year to attain optimum utilization of the fund and full enjoyment of benefits by the populace.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office submit the approved suspension and resumption order, if any or an explanation for the delay in project completion, otherwise collect the corresponding amount of liquidated damages from the contractor. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to withhold retention money from progress payments and ensure that only claims with complete supporting documents are processed and paid.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require that the programs, projects and activities be implemented during the budget year and the guidelines on the items to be charged to the LDRRMF be strictly followed. Likewise, require the Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statements the information pertaining to the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the rules and regulations regarding the attachment of the electronic copy of official receipts instead of an RER for car hires, taxi fares, rentals and other similar exp. as evidence of payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer of the LGU ascertain that requisitions for drugs and medicines conform with the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Vol. 1 and Joint DOH-DTI-IPO-BFAD Administrative Order No. 2008-01 prior to procurement. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries and hence-forth to record the correct account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the budget officer to submit justification for the excess of monetization of leave credits as against the appropriations. Henceforth, strictly incur expenses within the approved appropriation/budget. Likewise, require the personnel officer to immediately submit the lacking documents, and to require concerned officials to strictly observe the rules and regulations as well as the documentary requirements in processing application for monetization of leave credits.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to a Special Trust Fund and refrain from using the fund other than what was intended for pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Likewise, disclose the status of the fund in the Notes to Combined Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommended that a full-blown information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the Municipality’s constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations for the procurement of goods, services and equipment thru public bidding and resort to alternative methods of procurement only when the conditions for the adoption of same are met. In no case shall payments be made, unless they are duly supported with complete and appropriate procurement documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit to the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure projects or before the start of the programs/activity, a report that the appropriate project signboards and or public notices are already posted. Likewise, submit a report on the status of projects being implemented for monitoring purposes.
observations.reason: Appropriate project signboards or public notices were posted together with the DV. No report submitted on the status of projects being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be required to submit without delay all the forty-six (46) DVs and its related supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Unsubmitted thirteen (13) DVs for CY 2015 amounted to P405,202.82 as of Dec. 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports.
observations.reason: Some accounts and financial reports were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations on the submission of basic requirements applicable to all classes of disbursements and pertinent provisions for the prevention of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures.
observations.reason: Most of the financial transactions were supported with necessary documents. Some transactions were still subject for suspension.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to stop the practice of encashment of agency checks out of cash
collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant secure the list required
in Section 248 of RA 7160 for the period 2012 to 2016 and prepare adjusting
entries to correct the balances for the RPT and SET Receivables. Henceforth, a
duly certified list showing all the names of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible should be prepared by the Municipal Assessor and submitted to the
Municipal Treasurer as basis for recording by the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all
programs, projects and activities to be funded by the 20% Development Fund
are implemented during the budget year to attain optimum utilization of the
fund and full enjoyment of benefits by the populace.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office submit the
approved suspension and resumption order, if any or an explanation for the
delay in project completion, otherwise collect the corresponding amount of
liquidated damages from the contractor. Likewise, require the Municipal
Accountant to withhold retention money from progress payments and ensure
that only claims with complete supporting documents are processed and paid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the
programs, projects and activities be implemented during the budget year and
the guidelines on the items to be charged to LDRRMF be strictly followed.
Likewise, require the Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statements
the information pertaining to LDRRMF.

We recommended that the management through the Municipal Mayor
strictly observe the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 and its applicable IRR,
especially on the prohibition on splitting of contracts in its procurement
transactions. We also recommend that the General Services Office be required
to consolidate such requirements and for the BAC to conduct a quarterly
bidding thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the
rules and regulations regarding the attachment of the electronic copy of official
receipts instead of an RER for car hires, taxi fares, rentals and other similar
expenses as evidence of payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer of the LGU
ascertain that requisitions for drugs and medicines conform with the Philippine
National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Vol. 1 and Joint DOH-DTI-IPO-BFAD
Administrative Order No. 2008-01 dated November 8, 2008 prior to
procurement. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting
entries and henceforth to record the correct account in accordance with the
Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the budget officer
to submit justification for the excess of monetization of leave credits as against
the appropriations. Henceforth, strictly incur expenses within the approved
appropriation/budget.

Likewise, we recommended that the LCE direct the personnel officer to
immediately submit the lacking documents, and to require concerned officials to
strictly observe the rules and regulations as well as the documentary
requirements in processing application for monetization of leave credits.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local
Finance Committee to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent
to 1% of IRA to finance Local Council for the Protection of Children activities
and adopt a comprehensive plan and program for the welfare of the children.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the understatement of the Cash in Bank account at year-end. Henceforth, strictly observe the standards of honesty, objectivity and consistency in the keeping of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant already draw AAR journal entry voucher to correct the understatement of the Cash in Bank account at year-end.
observations.reason: The existence of unidentified reconciling item amounting P1,745,406.41

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant be directed to reconcile their records relative to collections and deposits, as well as cash advances and liquidation at least quarterly as provided for in the New Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the overstatement of the Cash Local Treasury and Advances for Payroll accounts.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the records with the cash receipts and disbursement and or cashbook and the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant were required to reconcile their records relative to collections and deposits, as well as cash advances and liquidation at least quarterly as provided for in the New Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant was required to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the overstatement of the Cash Local Treasury and Advances for Payroll accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that payments for financial assistance be made only when duly supported with complete and appropriate documents. Likewise, require the Social Welfare and Development Officer to formulate policies/guidelines on the grant of assistance to individuals in crisis situation and submit the same to the Sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the guidelines duly approved by the Sanggunian on the grant of assistance to individuals in crisis situation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to a Special Trust Fund and refrain from using the fund other than what was intended pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Likewise, disclose the status of fund in the Notes to Combined Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Transferred to a Special Trust Fund during CY 2017 amounting P4,528,671.52.
observations.reason: See related discussion on Finding No. 5, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal School Board prepare the Annual School Board Budget duly approved by its members and supported with programs, projects and activities as required.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the Annual School Board Budget for CY 2017 duly approved by its members and supported with programs, projects and activities as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a full-blown information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC conducted information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management program, so that the Municipality's Material Recovery Facility for Segregation and procured additional garbage truck for garbage collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and resort to some alternative methods of procurement only when the conditions for the adoption of same are met. In no case shall payments be made, unless they are duly supported with complete and appropriate procurement documents.
observations.managementAction: Management minimized its procurement through reimbursement and strictly adhered to RA 9184 (refer to Annex M).
observations.reason: Procurement during CY 2017 through reimbursement amounted to P203,287.27

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the officers and employees concerned be required to submit a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and properly filled up Official Driver’s Trip Tickets to document consumption of fuel, oil and lubricants. Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 in order to control and regulate the use of government vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and properly filled up Official Driver’s Trip Tickets and document consumption of fuel, oil and lubricants for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor submit to the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the projects or programs/activities, a report that the appropriate signboards and public notices are already posted. Likewise, submit a report on the status of projects being implemented for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: Submitted one (1) incomplete report for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be required to submit the forty-six (46) DVs in CY 2015 and its supporting documents to the Audit Team to remove the audit suspensions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant already submitted a substantial number of DVs for CY 2015 and its supporting documents amounting to P405,202.82 as of December 31, 2017.
observations.reason: Unsubmitted (13) thirteen DVs for CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor adopt a Contract of Service for each worker, instead of the Office Order, in order to include the basic and vital data required in any contract of service; and direct the person in-charge of preparation of the payrolls to include the accomplishment reports duly verified by officials having primary knowledge of the work performed by each JO and to attach the DTRs and accomplishment reports to the payrolls.
observations.managementAction: The contracts of service; accomplishment reports; and DTR were attached to the AAR payrolls submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant be required to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Not Submitted only one (1) incomplete report for CY 2017. However, delays were reduced from 22 days to 411 days in CY 2017 (refer to Annex O).
observations.reason: The distance of the municipality to the PSAO and the absence of vehicle to transport the documents caused the delayed submission of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal mayor require the municipal budget officer to strictly observe the 55% personal services limitation required in Section 325 of R.A. 7160 in the preparation of the annual personal budget.
observations.managementAction: The mayor represent incumbent employees holding permanent positon which cannot be removed from service without cause. Likewise, the budget for personal services was prepared.
observations.reason: The excess represent salaries of incumbent employees holding permanent position which cannot be removed from service without cause.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary journal adjustments to record the correct account balances, and to send demand letters to concerned employees with unliquidated cash advances. We also recommend that the Mayor strictly comply with submission of liquidation documents on his Intelligence and Confidential funds directly to the Chairman’s Office, COA, Quezon City without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Notices of Disallowance as a result of post-audit of transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded in the appropriate accounting journal all liquidations on the month that the liquidation documents were received. Likewise, Advances to Confidential Funds in CY 2011 and 2012 totalling P1,800,000.00 were reclassified to Advances to Special Disbursing Officer. The mayor was recommended to strictly comply the submission of liquidation documents on Intelligence and Confidential Funds directly to the Chairman’s Office, COA, Quezon City.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to adhere strictly with the rules and regulations on the subject applicable to all classes of disbursements and pertinent provisions for the prevention of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures.
observations.managementAction: AAR Partially implemented. Some transactions were still for submission of suspension and disallowance.
observations.reason: Some transactions were still for submission of suspension and disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a
journal entry voucher to correct the understatement of the Cash in Bank
account at year-end. Henceforth, strictly observe the standards of honesty,
objectivity and consistency in the keeping of accounts to safeguard against
inaccurate or misleading information.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be directed
to reconcile their records relative to collections and deposits, as well as cash
advances and liquidation at least quarterly as provided for in the New Cash
Examination Manual. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a
journal entry voucher to correct the overstatement of the Cash Local Treasury
and Advances for Payroll accounts. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant should strictly observe objectivity and consistency in the keeping of
accounts as provided for in Section 111 of PD 1445.

We recommend that payments for financial assistance be made only when
duly supported with complete and appropriate documents. Likewise, require
the Social Welfare and Development Officer to formulate policies/guidelines on
the grant of financial assistance to individuals in crisis situation and submit the
same to the Sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) RA 7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to transfer the
unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to a Special Trust Fund and refrain
from using the fund other than what was intended for pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Likewise, disclose the
status of fund in the Notes to Combined Financial Statements pursuant to
COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board be required to prepare
the Annual School Board Budget duly approved by its members and supported
with programs, projects and activities as required in Section 99 and 100(c) of
RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998.

We recommend that a full-blown information and dissemination campaign
on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the Municipality’s
constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA
9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations by procuring
goods, services and equipment thru public bidding and resort to alternative
methods of procurement only when the conditions for the adoption of same
are met. In no case shall payments be made, unless they are duly supported
with complete and appropriate procurement documents.

We recommend that the officers and employees concerned be required to
submit a Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption and properly filled up Driver’s Trip Tickets to document
consumption of fuel, oil and lubricants. Henceforth, strictly comply with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 in order to
control and regulate the use of government vehicles.
